content.full_text:
September 26, 1990

California Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Attention: Mr. Larson, Chairman

Dear Mr. Larson:

Following passage of Proposition 73, the California Fair Political Practices Commission established rules and regulations related to "Mass Mailings" and "Newsletters" prepared by local governmental agencies.

As a result of your rules and regulations, the Oxnard Harbor District, a Special Governmental District, established pursuant to the Harbors and Navigation Code and administered by a five member elected Board of Harbor Commissioners, modified the format of its "Port of Hueneme Newsletter" to delete any reference to the elected harbor commissioners within the newsletter. This was done to conform to the FPPC regulations for newsletters and mass mailings as interpreted by our Legal Counsel.

Recently, I received a copy of the City of Port Hueneme's newsletter in which the elected Port Hueneme City Council was identified. As a result of this newsletter, I have been requested to inquire whether the FPPC regulations allow the listing and identifications of elected harbor commissioners in our quarterly newsletter, similar to that of the City Council of the City of Port Hueneme.

California Fair Political Practices Commission
September 26, 1990
Page Two

If you need additional information, please feel free to contact me at (805)488-3677.

Respectfully,

[Signature]
Anthony J. Taormina
Executive Director

AJT:fp

cc: Each Commissioner
A. Whitfield
